exemplar.title: To what extent can Netflixâs success and growth be attributed to the corporate culture of freedom and feedback and Reed Hastingsâs leadership style, as well as the treatment of employees?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent can Netflixâs success and growth be attributed to the corporate culture of freedom and feedback and Reed Hastingsâs leadership style, as well as the treatment of employees?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and engagingly with comprehensive historical and contextual explanation
Research question is clearly stated, well-focused and consistently connected to the discussion
Appropriate range of relevant secondary sources and analytical tools are employed with evidence of informed selection
weaknesses: Rationale for source selection and credibility criteria is only briefly addressed
No primary research or systematic quantitative method to triangulate findings
Research question phrasing is slightly lengthy and could be refined for greater precision
Key term âsuccessâ is not operationally defined in the introduction
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place communication and question focus solidly in the top band and methodology in the upper-middle band. While the student demonstrates clear, comprehensive explanation and a wellâfocused question, minor omissions in methodological rationale and precision prevent the maximum mark. A score of 5 accurately reflects these strengths and remaining gaps.

strengths: Terminology and concepts (e.g. talent density, candour, SWOT) are used accurately and consistently
Source material is mostly relevant and appropriately integrated into arguments
Subject knowledge is clear and coherent, effectively applying organizational culture theories
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on Netflixâs own materials introduces confirmation bias
Several claims rest on single, unverified sources with missing page numbers
Coverage of counter-theories and critical engagement with alternative models is limited
Some uses of source material remain descriptive rather than analytical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (application of sources and subject knowledge) sit in the upper-middle band and one (use of terminology) in the top band. The work demonstrates solid knowledge and generally effective use of material but is moderated by bias risks and limited critical triangulation, warranting a mark of 4.

strengths: Sophisticated analysis tracing clear causal pathways and integrating theoretical frameworks
Excellent research quality with consistent relevance to the research question and effective triangulation
Well-supported conclusions with critical evaluation of benefits and risks
Arguments are logically coherent and demonstrate a high level of critical engagement
weaknesses: Lacks rigorous quantitative correlation analysis to bolster causal claims
Alternative academic viewpoints and counter-arguments are under-developed
Some evaluative comments remain somewhat general rather than deeply specific
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand scores of 10, 11 and 10 indicate performance at the upper end of the 10â12 band. The work exhibits excellent research quality and analysis depth, with only minor limitations in methodological transparency and counter-argumentation. An 11 reflects this high level of critical thinking while acknowledging small gaps.

strengths: All prescribed structural elements are present and logically ordered
Formatting largely follows IB norms with readable layout, numbered pages and consistent headings
Visuals are captioned and integrated appropriately
weaknesses: Table of contents omits page numbers and figure/table numbering is inconsistent
Reference list contains duplicate entries and lacks uniform citation details
Occasional misaligned tables and minor formatting lapses detract from full compliance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both structural and formatting strands sit at a solid level 3. The work meets core presentation requirements with coherent structure and generally correct layout, but inconsistencies and omissions prevent a top-band score. A mark of 3 appropriately reflects these observations.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research question is presented in the title and reiterated in the opening section.  It identifies the independent variables (Netflixâs culture of freedom/feedback and Reed Hastingsâs leadership) and the dependent variable (Netflixâs success and growth), indicating a clear cause-and-effect focus.  Throughout the analysis and SWOT sections, the student explicitly circles back to this question, maintaining coherence.

Nevertheless, the phrasing is lengthy and contains three linked drivers, which marginally dilutes precision; the notion of âtreatment of employeesâ is also partly subsumed by the other two drivers, hinting at some redundancy.  These minor focus issues justify a score of 5 rather than 6.

Arguments culminate in a balanced discussion that recognises both the catalytic effect of freedom-and-responsibility policies and the risks of high pressure, cultural misalignment and financial leakage.  The SWOT matrix is used as a platform for evaluation, and conclusions revisit the research question with clear linkage to preceding evidence.

Some evaluative comments remain general (e.g. âmany limitations may arise in the long runâ) and alternative academic viewpoints are under-developed, preventing the highest possible mark but still demonstrating an excellent, well-reasoned and critical approach worthy of a score of 10.

The student demonstrates secure knowledge of organisational-culture theory, leadership styles and human-resource practices, weaving Schein, laissez-faire leadership and talent-density concepts into the narrative.  Explanations are generally accurate and placed in an appropriate business context.

Nonetheless, coverage of counter-theories (e.g. Hofstede, contingency leadership) is limited, and quantitative measures of âgrowthâ are presented with minimal methodological reflection.  These gaps show clear but not fully developed subject knowledge, meriting a solid 4.

Terms such as âtalent densityâ, âcandourâ, âlaissez-faireâ, âSWOTâ, âturnoverâ, and âmarket capitalisationâ are used correctly and consistently.  Definitions are given when first introduced and later applied accurately in analysis and evaluation.  Occasional stylistic repetition does not undermine the precision and consistency displayed, placing this strand comfortably in the top band at a score of 5.

The analysis moves beyond description, tracing causal pathways from talent-density decisions in 2001 to share-price escalation, integrating Scheinâs deeper layers to explain behavioural outcomes, and weighing benefits against setbacks through SWOT.  Assertions are typically supported with data or authoritative quotation, and the student acknowledges limitations such as potential cultural mis-fit and spending leakage.

Sophistication is tempered slightly by an absence of rigorous quantitative correlation and limited scholarly counter-arguments, yet overall the depth, logical coherence and evidence support justify an upper-band score of 11.

Quotations, statistics and graphics are mostly relevant and integrated at the point of argument (e.g. share-price graph linked to policy milestones, direct citations from Hastings to illustrate âno-rulesâ ethos).  Referencing is generally sound and the material chosen clearly illuminates the cultural mechanisms under review.

Yet reliance on Hastingsâs own narrative introduces a degree of confirmation bias, and several claims (labour-turnover figure, cost-benefit of 10 % extra spend) rest on single, unverified sources.  Occasional missing page numbers and some descriptive rather than critical use moderate the strand to the upper-middle band.

A broad but generally relevant range of secondary sources is used â the Hastings & Meyer text, journalistic pieces, financial databases and academic models such as Schein.  The student explains the intention to triangulate insider and outsider perspectives and briefly acknowledges bias.  Business-management analytical tools (SWOT, Scheinâs levels, quantitative share-price trend) are appropriate to the question.

However, the rationale for source selection, criteria for credibility and limitations of exclusively secondary data are only touched upon.  No primary research or systematic quantitative method (e.g. correlation of culture metrics with KPIs) is attempted, so evidence of âeffective and informedâ methodological selection is partial rather than comprehensive.

The essay draws on a sizeable and pertinent evidence base: company financials, industry commentary, internal culture documents and academic texts.  Each data set is explicitly linked back to the research question, and the student frequently triangulates qualitative cultural insights with quantitative growth indicators.  While predominantly secondary, the breadth and relevance of sources demonstrate a consistently strong level of research quality; minor gaps in methodological transparency keep the mark to 10 rather than higher within the band.

The essay follows IB formatting norms: title and word count are provided, pages are numbered, and citations/ reference list largely adhere to a recognised style.  Visuals are included with captions and sources, and overall presentation is neat and readable.

Nonetheless, minor formatting lapsesâmissing page numbers in the contents, inconsistent citation details and occasional mis-aligned tablesâdetract from full compliance.  These shortcomings place the work at the lower end of the 3-4 band, warranting a score of 3.

The topic of Netflixâs leadership and culture is introduced early, contextualised with brief historical data and clearly linked to broader business-management themes such as talent density and empowerment.  Background statistics on market capitalisation, subscriber reach and geographic coverage immediately signal relevance and scale, while definitions of key constructs (e.g. âcorporate culture of freedom and feedbackâ) frame the investigation.

Explanation remains consistent across the essay, with repeated signposting that reminds the reader how each section connects to the central enquiry.  Minor shortcomings â for example, occasional repetition and the absence of an explicit operational definition for âsuccessâ in the introduction â prevent full marks, yet overall the communication is accurate, engaging and sufficiently comprehensive to sit firmly in the 5-6 band.

All prescribed structural elementsâtitle page, abstract substitute, contents, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion and reference listâare present and logically ordered.  Headings guide the reader and the flow of ideas is coherent.  However, the table of contents omits page numbers, figure/table numbering is inconsistent and a few section transitions are abrupt.  These issues indicate a generally appropriate but imperfect structure, situating the work at a solid 3 within the top band.

annotations.comment: Contrasting Netflixâs culture with highâstakes sectors (e.g. pharmaceuticals) is conceptually intriguing but unsupported; include examples or literature on sectors where controls remain essential.
The SWOT analysis lists strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats but lacks analysis of their interdependencies; expand to discuss how elements interact and influence outcomes.
The conclusion effectively summarizes findings but could be strengthened by offering practical recommendations or implications for managers applying Netflixâs cultural practices.
The reference list contains duplicate entries for the Kelly (2024) source; consolidate duplicates and ensure each work appears only once.
Several web references lack consistent access dates or DOIs; apply a uniform citation style (e.g. APA) and include all required publication details.
The report includes a clear and descriptive title at the start, aligning with IB formatting guidelines and orienting the reader to the research focus.
The essay aim statement references three linked drivers, which dilutes precision; consider narrowing variables to two or rephrasing to improve research question focus.

The plan to analyse quantitative metrics is stated without detailing analytical techniques (e.g. timeâseries methods, growthârate calculations); describe procedures to ensure transparency and replicability.
The term ânoârules rulesâ is used without an operational definition; provide a precise definition and source citation when introducing specialized concepts.
Policy evaluation cites a 10% cost increase from unrestricted spending but omits a net costâbenefit analysis; include quantitative ROI calculations to evaluate policy effectiveness.
The SWOT table is organized but would benefit from clearer formatting: ensure table numbering aligns with text and consider adding page references for each element.
The methodology lists secondary sources but lacks explicit criteria for source credibility and does not discuss limitations of secondaryâonly data; add justification of source selection.
The paragraph on candour ends midâsentence, indicating missing text; ensure complete sentences and continuity to maintain coherence and support your argument.
Background statistics rely heavily on Netflixâs own corporate materials, risking confirmation bias; integrate independent industry reports or academic analyses to triangulate data.
The methodology lists secondary sources but lacks explicit criteria for source credibility and does not discuss limitations of secondaryâonly data; add justification of source selection.
The paragraph on candour ends midâsentence, indicating missing text; ensure complete sentences and continuity to maintain coherence and support your argument.
Background statistics rely heavily on Netflixâs own corporate materials, risking confirmation bias; integrate independent industry reports or academic analyses to triangulate data.
The assertion that laissezâfaire leadership transformed Netflix into a global success omits counterâevidence; consider including examples where minimal controls led to challenges.

The introduction outlines Netflixâs history and scale but does not operationally define âsuccessâ; specify measurable indicators (e.g. revenue growth rate or subscriber milestones) to sharpen focus.

The plan to analyse quantitative metrics is stated without detailing analytical techniques (e.g. timeâseries methods, growthârate calculations); describe procedures to ensure transparency and replicability.
Discussion of Scheinâs organizational culture model remains descriptive; deepen analysis by evaluating its strengths and limitations in the dynamic, tech-driven context of Netflix.
The essay aim statement references three linked drivers, which dilutes precision; consider narrowing variables to two or rephrasing to improve research question focus.
The figure caption indicates data from 2005, but the narrative refers to share price data beginning in 2002; ensure consistency between text and visual sources to avoid misinterpretation.
Analysis of talent density cites Hastings & Meyer but would be strengthened by independent HR metrics (e.g. productivity or turnover statistics) to substantiate performance claims.
Discussion of candour and feedback is rich in quotations yet lacks empirical measures of impact; include data on engagement or performance improvements following feedback implementation.
The description of Netflixâs nonâanonymous 360âdegree feedback is thorough but should examine potential reluctance to critique senior staff and how this may affect candour.
Policy evaluation cites a 10% cost increase from unrestricted spending but omits a net costâbenefit analysis; include quantitative ROI calculations to evaluate policy effectiveness.
The table of contents lists main sections with page numbers but omits numbering for figures, tables, and subsections; include these for clearer navigation and structure consistency.
The student correctly provides the word count at the beginning of the document, satisfying the IB extended essay formatting requirement.

The introduction outlines Netflixâs history and scale but does not operationally define âsuccessâ; specify measurable indicators (e.g. revenue growth rate or subscriber milestones) to sharpen focus.
The report includes a clear and descriptive title at the start, aligning with IB formatting guidelines and orienting the reader to the research focus.
Theoretical discussion uses Tarverâs definition but would benefit from contrasting alternative culture frameworks (e.g. Hofstedeâs dimensions) to demonstrate broader subject knowledge.
The term ânoârules rulesâ is used without an operational definition; provide a precise definition and source citation when introducing specialized concepts.
Leadership style is summarized via CFI definitions but lacks critical evaluation of laissezâfaireâs potential drawbacks in highâstakes business decisions.
Noting an 8% turnover rate matching the market average contradicts claims of superior retention; discuss this anomaly and explore underlying factors.
The assertion that laissezâfaire leadership transformed Netflix into a global success omits counterâevidence; consider including examples where minimal controls led to challenges.
The student correctly provides the word count at the beginning of the document, satisfying the IB extended essay formatting requirement.
Analysis of talent density cites Hastings & Meyer but would be strengthened by independent HR metrics (e.g. productivity or turnover statistics) to substantiate performance claims.
The table of contents lists main sections with page numbers but omits numbering for figures, tables, and subsections; include these for clearer navigation and structure consistency.
Discussion of Scheinâs organizational culture model remains descriptive; deepen analysis by evaluating its strengths and limitations in the dynamic, tech-driven context of Netflix.
Theoretical discussion uses Tarverâs definition but would benefit from contrasting alternative culture frameworks (e.g. Hofstedeâs dimensions) to demonstrate broader subject knowledge.
The claim that dispersed decisionâmaking drives rapid innovation is asserted but lacks evidence; reference specific innovation outcomes or timeline correlations to support this link.
Leadership style is summarized via CFI definitions but lacks critical evaluation of laissezâfaireâs potential drawbacks in highâstakes business decisions.
The figure caption indicates data from 2005, but the narrative refers to share price data beginning in 2002; ensure consistency between text and visual sources to avoid misinterpretation.
The impact section reports revenue and shareâprice growth but does not link these trends to specific cultural or leadership changes; consider correlating key events with financial metrics.
Discussion of candour and feedback is rich in quotations yet lacks empirical measures of impact; include data on engagement or performance improvements following feedback implementation.
The description of Netflixâs nonâanonymous 360âdegree feedback is thorough but should examine potential reluctance to critique senior staff and how this may affect candour.
